case_name: RPY & Ors v Barnet, Enfield and Haringey Mental Health NHS Trust [2021] EWHC 180 (QB) (03 February 2021)

evidence_index.text: The purpose of today's hearing is for me to consider whether the proposed settlement of the damages claim in this case is in the best interests of the Second and Third Claimants as Protected Parties
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Defendant admitted that but for their negligence, the Father would not have taken his own life.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Over the four days before that happened, the Defendant accepts that there was no effective crisis intervention and that: an escalation of distress was not fully recognised; the treatment plan was vague and unacceptable; there was a failure to share information effectively; and there was no input from a psychiatrist.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Claimants' position in the proceedings is that had the Father received appropriate psychiatric care it is likely that he would have fully recovered
evidence_index.speaker: Claimants' position

evidence_index.text: An order in November 2019 entered judgment on liability, the Defendant having made admissions.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: I approve the settlement and make the Order in the terms discussed with both Counsel.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: I agree that is a sensible settlement, and a fair and appropriate apportionment, in the circumstances, from the Children's point of view.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Following invasive dental treatment, the patient experienced escalating pain and psychiatric symptoms over four days.

focused_exam.psychiatric: Escalation of distress not fully recognized

requestables.name: Psychiatric Evaluation
requestables.description: Consultation with a psychiatrist to assess mental health status

reveal.result_summary: No input from a psychiatrist documented
reveal.clinical_significance: Lack of psychiatric input contributed to inadequate care
reveal.decision_impact: Should have prompted a comprehensive psychiatric intervention

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with escalating psychiatric symptoms

key_events.event: Escalation of distress not fully recognized

decision_points.prompt: What should be the immediate management step?

options.description: Initiate crisis intervention with psychiatric input
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care for acute psychiatric crisis

options.description: Continue with vague treatment plan without psychiatric input
options.clinical_reasoning: Inadequate for the severity of symptoms

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant continued with vague treatment plan
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Treatment plan was vague and unacceptable

expected_action_court.description: Crisis intervention with psychiatric input should have been initiated
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established need for psychiatric care

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to provide effective crisis intervention

patient_outcome.description: Patient took his own life due to lack of effective crisis intervention

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to provide effective crisis intervention
Causation established - appropriate care would have prevented the outcome

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to provide effective crisis intervention
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Appropriate psychiatric care would have led to recovery

allegations.allegation: Failure to provide effective crisis intervention
